name: Apple Brioche Calvados Cake
recipeIngredient: 4 pounds Granny Smiths, peeled; thinly sliced
1 pound Brioche, cut into 1-inch thick slices
1 pound Granny Smiths, peeled; thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook 2-1/2 cups sugar in a large heavy skillet over low heat until dissolved, without stirring. Swirl the pan occasionally.
2. Add 4 pounds of apples and cook until tender and lightly caramelized, about an hour. Cool.
3. Scald milk.
4. In a bowl, whisk together eggs, 2/3 cup sugar, and Calvados.
5. Whisk the mixture into the milk.
6. Place brioche in a flat pan large enough to hold it and pour the milk/egg mixture over it. Let set long enough so the brioche absorbs it all.
7. Melt 1 cup butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add brioche in batches and brown well on both sides.
8. Butter an 8-inch springform pan. Using about three-quarters of the brioche, line the bottom and sides of the pan.
9. Spoon cooked apples atop the brioche.
10. Cover apples with remaining brioche.
11. Top with a plate and weight with a small can from your cupboard. Refrigerate at least 3 hours, or overnight.
12. When almost ready to serve, cook the last 1 cup sugar in a heavy skillet over low heat until dissolved, swirling pan occasionally.
13. Increase heat to medium, add apples and cook until lightly caramelized, about 25 minutes.
14. To serve, remove sides of springform pan. Top with hot apples and garnish with either rich vanilla ice cream or lightly sweetened whipped cream.
